retry_reason: refcount_dec is unconditional and never fails; the put wrapper is a release operation and must only be called when a reference is held—double-put is a use-after-free bug, so retry makes no sense.
contract_summary: Decrements the reference count of an inet6_dev object. The caller must already hold a valid reference (typically obtained via a prior get or allocation). After this call, if the reference count reaches zero, the object may be freed and must not be accessed again. The function does not return any value; the caller must ensure proper pairing with a corresponding get or init. This is a pure release-side wrapper with no special locking or RCU requirements beyond those imposed by the caller's reference discipline.
